data.content:
Now I have the full picture. Here's my analysis of all legacy patterns, followed by the modernized project.

**Legacy patterns found:**

| # | Pattern | Resolution |
|---|---------|------------|
| 1 | Non-SDK `<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="...">` | → `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` imports | Removed — SDK handles automatically |
| 3 | Default `Configuration`/`Platform` properties | Removed — SDK defaults |
| 4 | `ProjectGuid` | Removed — not needed in SDK-style |
| 5 | `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic` | Removed — already SDK defaults |
| 6 | `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | Removed — not needed |
| 7 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | → `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| 8 | Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | Removed — SDK provides sensible defaults |
| 9 | Explicit framework references (System, System.Core, etc.) | Removed — implicit in SDK |
| 10 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | Removed — SDK uses automatic glob |
| 11 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Removed — metadata moves to `<PropertyGroup>` |

Applying changes now:

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Product>LegacyApp</Product>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize it.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Non-SDK `<Project>` element** — uses `ToolsVersion="15.0"` and the `xmlns` attribute instead of `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"`
2. **Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` imports** — SDK-style handles these automatically
3. **Default `Configuration` and `Platform` properties** — SDK defaults handle these
4. **`ProjectGuid`** — not needed in SDK-style


5. **`FileAlignment`** — default, not needed
6. **`AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`** — not relevant for .NET Core/modern TFMs
7. **`Deterministic`** — already the default in SDK-style
8. **Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks** — SDK-style has sensible defaults for these (DebugSymbols, DebugType, Optimize, OutputPath, DefineConstants, ErrorReport, WarningLevel)
9. **Explicit framework assembly references** (System, System.Core, etc.) — not needed, SDK handles this
10. **Explicit `<Compile Include` patterns** — SDK-style automatically includes all source files by default
11. **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — assembly attributes are auto-generated from project properties in SDK-style
12. **`TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2** — should become `TargetFramework` with the modern TFM moniker `net472` (or upgrade to .NET 6/8)

So I'll convert this to SDK-style by rewriting the `.csproj`, removing `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` since those attributes now live in the project file, and likely deleting the entire `Properties/` directory. For the target framework, I'll stick with `net472` unless they want to modernize to .NET 6 or 8.

data.content:
Done. The project went from **47 lines → 14 lines**. Here's a summary of what changed:

- **`.csproj` rewritten** as SDK-style (`Microsoft.NET.Sdk`) — all boilerplate removed
- **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` deleted** — metadata (`Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version`) moved into `<PropertyGroup>`
- **`OutputType`** omitted — `Library` is the SDK default
- **Framework references** (System, System.Core, etc.) removed — implicit
- **`<Compile>` items** removed — SDK auto-globs `**/*.cs`
- **Debug/Release configurations** removed — SDK provides equivalent defaults
- **Target framework** preserved as `net472` (same as the original `v4.7.2`)
